changes_summary: Reworked Step 3 to fix the flagged correctness bug. Previously the plan defaulted to calling `onActionResizeEnd` twice from `TimelineCanvas`, but `useClipResize` keeps per-clip `resizeStartRef` (from/to/speed origin) and `resizeTransactionIdRef`, so the neighbor would never get a recorded origin and meta math would be wrong, plus the undo step would split. New plan extends both `onActionResizeStart` and `onActionResizeEnd` in `useClipResize.ts` to accept an optional `adjacent` clip, records both clips' origins on start under one shared transaction id, and commits both via a single `applyEdit` on end. Step 4 (TimelineCanvas) now resolves the neighbor at `handleResizePointerDown`, fires the start/end callbacks once each with `adjacent` populated, and skips `snapResize` only when an interior group neighbor is engaged. Added Step 3 as a dedicated hook-extension step (was previously folded into a paragraph in old Step 3). Updated test expectations in Step 5 to match the single-callback contract. Removed the resolved transaction-batching question. Promoted the single-undo-step criterion from `should` to `must`. Added a `must` for the type widening reaching all consumers (`useTimelineState.types.ts`, `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, `useTimelineState.contexts.ts`).
questions: When the dragged edge would shrink the neighbor below `minDuration`, should the drag clamp (neighbor stops at minDuration, dragged edge halts) or should the neighbor be allowed to shrink past the visible minimum? The plan assumes clamp-the-drag.

For resizes where the right edge of the LAST clip or left edge of the FIRST clip is dragged (free outer edges), should we also skip snapping to OTHER group siblings? The plan preserves existing snap behavior on free outer edges — confirm that's fine.

success_criteria.criterion: When a clip is inside a pinned shot group, right-clicking it shows only 'Update to current variant' and/or 'Dismiss reminder' (as applicable) and no Split Here / Delete Clip / Create Shot / Generate Video / existing shots list.
When a clip is inside a pinned shot group and has neither an available variant update nor a dismissable stale reminder, right-clicking does not open a context menu at all.
The amber stale-variant badge click is also gated when `isInPinnedShotGroup` and no asset-state actions are available.
When a clip is NOT inside a pinned shot group, the context menu behaves exactly as before (no regressions).

Resizing the left edge of a middle-or-last clip inside a pinned shot group moves the previous clip's right edge to match, so the group remains contiguous and `positionedShotGroups` gap validation still renders the group overlay.
Resizing the right edge of a middle-or-first clip inside a pinned shot group moves the next clip's left edge to match.
Resizing the free outer edge (left of first clip, right of last clip) inside a pinned shot group does not shift any sibling.
Adjacent-clip auto-shift respects the existing minimum-duration constraint and clamps the *primary* drag rather than collapsing the neighbor.

A resize that shifts two clips inside a pinned shot group produces a SINGLE undo step (one `applyEdit` with one shared `transactionId`), and BOTH clips' meta (`from`/`to`/`speed`) is computed from their pre-drag origin recorded at `onActionResizeStart`.
`onActionResizeStart` is called with the `adjacent` field populated whenever the resize will auto-shift a neighbor, so `useClipResize` records both origins before drag begins.

Type widening of `onActionResizeStart`/`End` reaches all consumers (`useClipResize.ts`, `useTimelineState.types.ts`, `useTimelineState.contexts.ts`, `VideoEditorProvider.tsx`, `TimelineEditor.tsx`, `TimelineCanvas.tsx`) and the project type-checks clean.
Resizing clips OUTSIDE a pinned shot group is unchanged: existing sibling snap still works, single `onActionResizeEnd` call with `adjacent` undefined.
All existing tests in `ClipAction.test.tsx` and `TimelineCanvas.test.tsx` pass (excluding the pre-existing unrelated `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` failure).

New tests cover: gated menu with and without asset-state actions; gated stale-badge click; auto-shift left and right interior edges (single callback with `adjacent`); free-edge resize (single callback, `adjacent` undefined); minDuration clamp on neighbor.
No files are touched outside `src/tools/video-editor/` (scope guard per brief).
No new toasts, no DB/migration/edge function changes; CLAUDE.md conventions followed (no hardcoded colors, shadcn patterns preserved).
`ClipAction.tsx` does not grow significantly in LOC beyond the minimum needed for the new gating.
The `useClipResize` end callback issues exactly one `applyEdit` regardless of whether `adjacent` is present (avoids dataRef-staleness between two sequential commits).
Manual smoke: pin a 3-clip shot group, resize middle clip edges, verify label stays visible, neighbor edges follow, single undo restores both edges; right-click each clip and confirm gating.

assumptions: Extending `useClipResize.onActionResizeStart`/`End` with an optional `adjacent` field is the right interface change; the existing single-clip call sites (drags outside any pinned group) keep compiling unchanged.

`resolveOverlaps` invoked on the merged `rows` after applying the primary's edge produces correct results when then re-invoked for the adjacent clip in the same callback (i.e., the helper is referentially safe to call twice within one end-callback). If not, the helper extraction should compose row updates before a single overlap pass.
`shotGroups` prop on `TimelineCanvas` lists clips in left-to-right order within each group (consistent with `positionedShotGroups` iteration expecting `current.start >= previous.end`).
`isInPinnedShotGroup` suppression also applies to the amber stale-variant badge click handler (`ClipAction.tsx:410`) for consistency.
The pre-existing `data-shot-group-kind='pinned'` test failure is genuinely unrelated; we will not touch it.
`minDuration` for the adjacent clip is the same component-level `minDuration` used in `updateResize`.

The typescript widening of `onActionResizeStart`/`End` will need to flow through `useTimelineState.types.ts:85`, `useTimelineState.contexts.ts:139,205`, `VideoEditorProvider.tsx:107`, and `TimelineEditor.tsx:170` — all in the video-editor folder, so still scope-clean.
